steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to "provide an example of a country that uses the word 'dollar' in the name of its currency." A valid answer must (1) name a specific country and (2) show that country's currency uses the word "dollar." General claims, negative statements, and etymology do not satisfy either requirement.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Kiribati uses the Kiribati dollar as its national currency.': Names a specific country (Kiribati) and explicitly shows its currency uses "dollar." Directly accomplishes the goal. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Kiribati does not refer to its currency by the German word "thaler."': A negative statement — it says what Kiribati does not do. This provides no example of a currency that uses "dollar." ✗
  • Choice C — 'The German word "thaler" is the origin of the English word "dollar."': Pure etymology with no country and no currency name — irrelevant to the goal. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Today, many countries use the word "dollar" in naming their currencies.': A vague generalization about "many countries" with no specific example — fails the goal. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
